document.title: Qualification Extension Catalog — Heritage Gold → LAWIM_V2
document.description: Enriched catalog of all qualification engine extensions — 107 matrices, field dictionary, priority system, progressive 10-step order, per-channel adaptation, versioning, and applicability rules

extensions.current_limitation: No qualification matrix system exists in V2. Property qualification is handled via free-form project_type selection with no structured field collection.
extensions.proposed_structure: Create QualificationMatrixDefinition entity as versioned metadata (not rigid columns). Store matrix definitions in database with codes RES-APT-BUY through RES-OTH-RENT. Each matrix links to N QualificationFieldDefinitions (15-26 fields per matrix), QualificationRuleDefinitions, ReadinessDefinitions, and MatrixApplicability rules. Matrix versions stored as immutable snapshots in MatrixVersion entity. Qualification answers stored as JSON document (qualification_data) on Project entity.
required_permissions: QUAL: read matrix definitions (all authenticated users)

extensions.current_limitation: No land-specific qualification matrices exist. Land property type exists but has no differentiated qualification fields or title status handling.
extensions.proposed_structure: Implement 7 land qualification matrices as QualificationMatrixDefinition records with domain='land'. Each matrix includes land-specific fields: title_status (deal_breaker), is_constructible (hard_constraint), land_area (strong_preference), flood_zone (deal_breaker). Matrices differentiated by transaction type (buy vs invest) and land subtype (plot, agricultural_land, commercial_land, industrial_land).
required_fields: QualificationFieldDefinition: title_status, is_constructible, land_area, land_use, road_access, electricity_nearby, water_nearby, flood_zone, land_orientation

extensions.current_limitation: No commercial qualification matrices exist. Commercial property types are not differentiated from residential in qualification flow.
extensions.proposed_structure: Implement 21 commercial qualification matrices as QualificationMatrixDefinition records with domain='commercial'. Each matrix includes commercial-specific fields: commercial_type, front_window, ceiling_height, floor_load, loading_dock, parking_capacity, access_truck, visibility, commercial_zone, license_type. Matrices cover office, shop, warehouse, hotel, garage, hangar, laboratory, club_hall, and multi-commercial types across buy, rent, and commercial_lease transaction types.
required_fields: QualificationFieldDefinition: commercial_type, front_window, ceiling_height, floor_load, loading_dock, parking_capacity, access_truck, visibility, commercial_zone, license_type

extensions.current_limitation: No investment-specific qualification matrices exist. Investment intent is not differentiated in qualification flow.
extensions.proposed_structure: Implement 5 investment qualification matrices as QualificationMatrixDefinition records with domain='investment'. Each matrix includes investment-specific fields: investment_type, roi_target, investment_horizon, monthly_rental_income, management_type, exit_strategy, co_investors. Matrices differentiate by investment target: residential, commercial, land, project development, and condominium syndicate.
required_fields: QualificationFieldDefinition: investment_type, roi_target, investment_horizon, monthly_rental_income, management_type, exit_strategy, co_investors

extensions.current_limitation: No financing qualification matrices exist. Financing requests are not handled as a standalone qualification path.
extensions.proposed_structure: Implement 10 financing qualification matrices as QualificationMatrixDefinition records with domain='financing'. Each matrix includes financing-specific fields: financing_type, loan_amount, down_payment, loan_duration, monthly_payment, interest_rate, employment_status, monthly_income, existing_loans, guarantor_available. Matrices cover mortgage (buy/construction/renovation), personal loan, lease, investment financing, construction loan, bridge loan, top-up loan, and social housing financing.
required_fields: QualificationFieldDefinition: financing_type, loan_amount, down_payment, loan_duration, monthly_payment, interest_rate, employment_status, monthly_income, existing_loans, guarantor_available

extensions.current_limitation: No professional search matrices exist. FIND-type project has no structured qualification for professional service discovery.
extensions.proposed_structure: Implement 27 professional search matrices as QualificationMatrixDefinition records with domain='professional'. Each matrix represents a distinct professional type (agent, notary, surveyor, architect, engineer, electrician, plumber, mason, carpenter, painter, tiler, roofer, real estate expert, appraiser, condo manager, drone videographer, broker, security, admin provider, lawyer, accountant, insurance, banker, developer, decorator, gardener, pool builder). Includes professional search fields: professional_type, service_required, city_pro, neighborhood_pro, language_pro, years_experience, certification, urgency_pro.
required_fields: QualificationFieldDefinition: professional_type, service_required, city_pro, neighborhood_pro, language_pro, years_experience, certification, urgency_pro

extensions.source_concept: Real Estate Service qualification matrices (24 matrices)
extensions.current_limitation: No real estate service qualification matrices exist. SERVICE-type project has no structured qualification for service procurement.
extensions.proposed_structure: Implement 24 real estate service qualification matrices as QualificationMatrixDefinition records with domain='service'. Each matrix represents a distinct real estate service type: estimation, expertise, document verification, visit, second visit, rental management, rental listing, sales listing, publication, photography, videography, drone, home staging, renovation, construction, maintenance, cleaning, security, moving, insurance, legal advice, tax advice, condo management, rent recovery. Includes service request fields: service_type, property_to_service, city_service, service_description, service_timeline, service_budget.
required_fields: QualificationFieldDefinition: service_type, property_to_service, city_service, service_description, service_timeline, service_budget

extensions.current_limitation: No field-to-role mapping exists in V2. Qualification fields are not mapped to matching engine roles, making qualification data unusable for automated matching.
extensions.proposed_domain: Domain 5: Dossier/Project Model + Domain 6: Matching Engine
extensions.proposed_structure: Create QualificationFieldDefinition entity as the canonical field dictionary. Each field maps to a MatchingSemanticDefinition that declares: matching_role (hard_constraint, strong_preference, weak_preference, nice_to_have, deal_breaker, tie_breaker, exclusion, boost, transaction_blocker), weight (0.0-1.0), scoring_function (exact_match, range_match, fuzzy_match, boolean_match, enum_match, geographic_distance), boost_value (for boost role), penalty_value (for mismatch penalties), is_filter (pre-filter before scoring), filter_operator, and exclusion_values. Each field also declares its priority (mandatory, important, optional), step_number (1-10), per-channel visibility flags, and conditional dependency rules. Core fields (intention, property_type, city, budget_min, budget_max) are shared across all matrices. Domain-specific fields are defined per matrix family.
required_states: QualificationFieldDefinition.matching_role ∈ {hard_constraint, strong_preference, weak_preference, nice_to_have, deal_breaker, tie_breaker, exclusion, boost, transaction_blocker}

extensions.current_limitation: No question priority weighting in V2. All qualification questions are treated equally with no differentiation between mandatory, important, and optional fields.
extensions.proposed_structure: Implement 3-tier priority system on QualificationFieldDefinition: mandatory (must answer, cannot proceed without), important (should answer, encouraged but deferrable), optional (nice to have, can skip freely). Priority determines: (1) asking order — mandatory first, then important, then optional; (2) channel behavior — WhatsApp only asks mandatory (1 per interaction), Telegram asks mandatory + 1 important, Dashboard renders all; (3) matching impact — mandatory fields act as pre-filters, important fields are scoring inputs, optional fields are boost-only. Priority distribution varies by domain: residential 4-6 mandatory / 5-8 important / 6-12 optional; land 5-7 / 4-6 / 4-8; commercial 5-7 / 6-10 / 6-14; investment 4-5 / 5-7 / 3-6; financing 5-7 / 5-8 / 3-6; professional 3-4 / 3-5 / 4-8; service 3-4 / 3-5 / 3-6.
required_fields: QualificationFieldDefinition.sort_order: Int (display order within priority tier)
QuestionRuleDefinition.max_retries: Int (3 for mandatory, 1 for important, 0 for optional)

important.behavior: User can defer but is encouraged
important.whatsapp: aggregated into 'we need a few more details'

asking_order.step_10: Confirmation of all answers + escalation path

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has flat journey steps with no progressive disclosure order. All questions are presented simultaneously regardless of context or channel.
extensions.proposed_structure: Implement 10-step progressive qualification order as a strict state machine on Project. Step 1 (Intention): Determine primary intent. Step 2 (Type): Property/Service type classification. Step 3 (Ville): City selection. Step 4 (Quartier): Neighborhood preference. Step 5 (Budget): Budget range. Step 6 (Délai): Timeline and urgency. Step 7 (Critères): Specific criteria (surface, bedrooms, condition). Step 8 (Préférences): Preferences (furnished, parking, etc.). Step 9 (Confirmation): Summary confirmation. Step 10 (Escalade): Escalate to agent if incomplete. Each step has per-channel question limits (WhatsApp: 1, Telegram: 2-3, Dashboard: full). Transition rules: Step N → N+1 when all mandatory fields in Step N are answered. Step N → N+2 when user skips all optional fields. Any step → Step 10 when user requests agent assistance. State tracked via Project.qualification_step (Int 1-10). Step definitions stored as QualificationRuleDefinition records with rule_type='step_transition'.
required_permissions: QUAL: read qualification step (all project participants)
QUAL: update qualification step (project owner, agent)

transition_rules.condition: All mandatory fields in Step N are answered
User explicitly says 'I don't know' or skips all optional fields
Confirmation = true AND all mandatory fields answered
Confirmation = false OR mandatory fields missing
User requests agent assistance at any point
User provides invalid input (max 3 retries per field)

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has single qualification flow regardless of channel. WhatsApp, Telegram, and Dashboard all present the same questions in the same format, leading to poor UX on chat channels.
extensions.proposed_domain: Domain 5: Dossier/Project Model + Channel Integration Layer
extensions.proposed_structure: Implement channel-adaptive qualification engine that renders the same matrix differently per channel. Three channel profiles: (1) WhatsApp: 1 question per interaction, session-based (24h timeout), quick reply buttons, free text fallback, agent handoff via 'Talk to agent' keyword. (2) Telegram: 2-3 questions per interaction, inline keyboard, session-based (48h timeout), /agent command for escalation. (3) Dashboard: full step rendered at once, form inputs with validation, step indicator, progress tracking, persistent session. Per-channel question limits: WhatsApp — mandatory 1, important 0 (aggregated), optional 0; Telegram — mandatory 2, important 1, optional 0 (expandable); Dashboard — all fields rendered. Channel adaptation configuration stored on QuestionRuleDefinition with fields: channel (whatsapp|telegram|dashboard|api), question_template (multilingual), response_type, max_questions_per_interaction, min_answers_before_proceed, allow_skip, auto_advance, confirmation_required. Each QualificationFieldDefinition also has is_visible_on_whatsapp, is_visible_on_telegram, is_visible_on_dashboard flags.
required_relations: QuestionRuleDefinition mapped to channel integration service
required_permissions: QUAL: render qualification (per-channel — any user on their own project)
required_states: QuestionRuleDefinition.response_type ∈ {free_text, single_choice, multiple_choice, number, yes_no, location_picker, date_picker}
extensions.decision_question: Per-channel question limits: Gold defaults (WhatsApp:1, Telegram:2-3, Dashboard:full) or adjustable per channel per matrix?

whatsapp.question_format: Single question with quick reply buttons
whatsapp.error_handling: 'I didn't understand. Please reply with a number.'

dashboard.question_format: Form with validation, help tooltips, sliders
dashboard.progress_indication: Step indicator at top of form
